definition: Commodity price increases transmit more readily to consumer prices than decreases — the rockets and feathers phenomenon. The 2014-15 oil price collapse barely moved core CPI downward, while comparable magnitude increases produce measurable CPI effects.
evidence: 2014-15 oil collapse barely moved core CPI despite comparable magnitude to upward shocks
Documented across 5 historical episodes by generalist_02
Consistent with firm pricing behavior — stickier downward adjustment
open_questions: What is the quantitative magnitude of the asymmetry?
Does the asymmetry vary by commodity type or transmission stage?
Is the asymmetry increasing or decreasing over time?
